package fixture

import "context"

// RegionDispatch models the B1.5/B5.1 region-dispatch idiom (design §3.2):
// Encode dispatches to same-receiver encodeJMS/encodeGMS helper methods that
// take the passed *response.Writer and write via it. The analyzer must descend
// into the helpers and inline their writes under the dispatch guard, rather
// than treating the unresolved m.encodeGMS(w) call as a no-op (which collapses
// every field to byte → false ❌).
type RegionDispatch struct{}

func (m RegionDispatch) Encode(l interface{}, ctx context.Context) func(map[string]interface{}) []byte {
	w := newWriter()
	t := mustTenant(ctx)
	return func(opts map[string]interface{}) []byte {
		if t.Region() == "JMS" {
			m.encodeJMS(w)
		} else {
			m.encodeGMS(w)
		}
		return w.Bytes()
	}
}

func (m RegionDispatch) encodeGMS(w *Writer) {
	w.WriteInt(0)
	w.WriteInt(1)
}

func (m RegionDispatch) encodeJMS(w *Writer) {
	w.WriteInt(2)
}
